steps.text: Direct questions regarding appropriate collaborative activities to the Office of the General Counsel (OGC), CDC Branch, Department of Health & Human Services (HHS).
Refer questions and issues relating to gifts to individual employees from organizations outside the federal government to the Human Resources Office (HRO), CDC Ethics and Compliance Activity.
Deposit the money in the general fund of the Treasury as soon as practicable.

preconditions.text: If there are questions regarding appropriate collaborative activities
If there are questions and issues relating to gifts to individual employees from organizations outside the federal government
If an official or agent of the Government receives money for the Government from any source

constraints.text: Gifts from foreign governments or international organizations, if authorized by the agency, are subject to the Foreign Gifts and Decorations Act (5 U.S.C. Section 7342).
An agency may not augment its appropriation from an outside source without specific statutory authority to do so.

steps.text: Deposit the money in the general fund of the Treasury as soon as practicable.
Accept monetary gifts in lieu of gifts in kind.

preconditions.text: An agency accepts money from an outside source, and the funds do not constitute an authorized repayment, and the agency lacks specific statutory authority to retain the funds.
Accepting monetary gifts in lieu of gifts in kind is feasible.

constraints.text: Do not accept gifts if the conditions imposed by the donor are illegal, contrary to public policy, unreasonable to administer, contrary to generally accepted public standards, an actual conflict of interest, or an apparent conflict of interest.

steps.text: Determine whether acceptance of a gift would compromise or appear to compromise the integrity of CDC or its employees, require CDC to undertake activities unrelated to its mission, or exert influence over its priorities.
Consider the identity of the immediate donor and, if applicable, the identity of any entity that funds the donor.
Review the gift for conflicts of interest through the established CDC process.

constraints.text: An agency may not accept for its own use gifts of money or other property in the absence of specific statutory authority.

steps.text: Prepare letters of acceptance for a conditional gift.
Propose project ideas to the CDC Foundation and cultivate partnerships around program opportunities.
Accept and utilize, on behalf of the federal government, any gift, donation, bequest, or devise of real or personal property from the CDC Foundation.
Conduct a conflict-of-interest review of the potential donor prior to accepting the gift.
Provide CDC with relevant information.

preconditions.text: Gift funds must be collected in advance of use.

constraints.text: CDC may not direct the CDC Foundation to make a gift to CDC.

steps.text: Transfer proceeds from the liquidation of personal property to OFR within 24 hours after receipt for deposit with the Secretary of the Treasury.
Request that the executor liquidate the assets and make distribution in cash.
Forward copies of wills and trusts naming CDC as beneficiary and letters of transmittal to OGC.
Acknowledge receipt of the will or trust promptly.
Notify the Deputy Chief Financial Officer (CFO) when the estate is settled.

preconditions.text: The bequest or legacy is all or part of a general estate of a deceased individual.

constraints.text: Funds received by or for the United States Government or its agencies must be deposited in, and administered through, United States Government accounts and may not be deposited with or otherwise administered by other persons or organizations.

steps.text: Begin the conflict of interest review within the CIO.
Submit the gift to the CDC Conflict of Interest Review Panel for review.

preconditions.text: The gift is a monetary gift over $500.

constraints.text: All gifts subject to this policy must undergo conflict of interest review at CDC.
CDC Foundation gifts must undergo a separate, independent conflict of interest review by the CDC Foundation.

steps.text: Conduct a center-level conflict of interest review, documenting the review, the donor, and a description of the in-kind gift.
Estimate the monetary value of the gift, if possible, and record it in the review documentation.
Consult with OGC.
Request a CDC COI Panel Review if further elevation is required.
Follow the abbreviated emergency response conflict of interest review process.

preconditions.text: A CIO makes a determination that there is a potential conflict of interest concern.
The Emergency Operations Center (EOC) is activated for a public health emergency.

steps.text: Follow the abbreviated emergency response conflict of interest review process.
Conduct a review from OGC and the Ethics Desk of the EOC.
Complete a Gift Acceptance Validity Survey (Appendix 2) by the Office of the Director.
Relay any further considerations back to OGC, the Ethics Desk, and the CDC Foundation as appropriate.
Follow a conflict-of-interest review process for potential gifts and donors during an emergency response (CDC Foundation).
Subject the gift to an extra layer of scrutiny, including completion of the Gift Acceptance Validity Survey (Appendix 2), before potential acceptance.

preconditions.text: There is a potential Prohibited Source, or there is need for additional review.
Gifts are offered by a prohibited source (whether a CDC Foundation gift or a direct gift).

constraints.text: A prohibited source is any individual or entity that: seeks official action by CDC, does business or seeks to do business with CDC, conducts activities regulated by CDC, has interests substantially affected by performance/nonperformance of duties, benefits from CDC work to promote business, or is an organization with a majority of such members.

steps.text: Contact OFR, Office of Debt Management regarding gift returns.
Ensure written notification accompanies all gift returns.
Contact the CDC Ethics and Compliance Activity for guidance regarding disposition of gifts to individual employees.
Return tangible items to the donor.
Pay the donor market value (retail cost of an item or service of like quality).
Destroy, share within the office, or donate items.

preconditions.text: If a gift cannot be accepted
Subject to OGC approval as a legally permissible use of appropriated funds
Subject to approval of a supervisor or organization head

constraints.text: Employees may use first-class mail for the limited purpose of forwarding reimbursements to donors.

CDC will not accept a gift that is conditioned upon any expenditure that cannot be covered by the gift itself or from income earned from the gift, unless an Act of Congress has approved such expenditure.
Offers of property will not be accepted if the total costs associated with acceptance are expected to exceed the cost of purchasing a similar item and the cost of normal care and maintenance.
Gifts may not be accepted if the conditions placed on the gift create a conflict of interest.

steps.text: Advise donors from commercial organizations that the acceptance of a gift does not constitute an implied or direct endorsement.
Examine the precise terms of the statute authorizing the agency to accept the gift to evaluate the propriety of a proposed use of gift funds.
Determine if the proposed use of gift funds conflicts with the terms of the agency's authorizing statute.
Follow the limitations imposed by the authorizing statute when expending gift funds.

preconditions.text: It is determined that the proposed use will not conflict with the terms of the agency's authorizing statute.

constraints.text: Gifts tendered in order to secure an implied or direct endorsement are not accepted.
Gifts may not be accepted when the donor expresses, directly or indirectly, an expectation of receiving a future benefit, such as a contract award from CDC.
CDC officials cannot accept conditional gifts of real property; gifts of real property may only be accepted by the Secretary, HHS/ASFR.
Gift funds can only be used to further authorized agency purposes and in accordance with the terms of the donation.

steps.text: Obtain pre-approval from OFR for gift fund expenditures for entertainment.
Verify that the expenditure meets the necessary expense test.
Spend the gift funds in the same manner as appropriated funds.

preconditions.text: The entertainment is consistent with the purposes of the gift.
The entertainment furthers a valid, authorized function of CDC, or the function could not be otherwise accomplished without the expenditure, or the expenditure does not violate donor restrictions.

constraints.text: Donated funds may not be used for entertainment that does not bear a legitimate official purpose, such as an office holiday party.
Once received by CDC, gift funds become public funds and must be spent in the same manner as appropriated funds.

steps.text: Consult with the OFR or the Ethics and Compliance Activity Office for assistance with sponsored travel.
Determine whether the funds are intended for CDC or for the employee serving in an individual capacity.
Accept the funds as a gift and deposit them into the appropriate CDC gift fund account.
Seek guidance as necessary from the CDC Ethics and Compliance Activity to avoid potential conflicts.

preconditions.text: If dealing with sponsored travel (travel which has some or all costs paid for in part or completely by a non-Federal Source).
If unsolicited grants or other types of awards are made to CDC employees for scientific or other professional purposes.
If an employee receives funds from any outside organization that supports the costs of CDC activities.

constraints.text: All statutory requirements regarding the acceptance of conditional gifts must be met.
Grant funds must be used to advance CDC’s mission, and there must be no legal objections to CDC’s acceptance of these grants.
Such a gift should not be a gift to the employee.

steps.text: Add property CDC receives as a gift to CDC property records.
Notify OFR of changes in the status of property that should be reflected in the general ledger.
Assign monetary or gifts in kind received by the Office of the Director to the referenced CIO for coordinated handling.
Handle the responsibilities as outlined for CIOs (by the Office of the Chief of Staff).

preconditions.text: When a CIO is not specified for a gift

constraints.text: Offers of personal property will not be accepted if the total costs associated with acceptance are expected to exceed the cost of purchasing a similar item and the cost of normal care and maintenance.
Exercise the same degree of custody and control over this property as all other government-owned property.

steps.text: Forward the cash or cash equivalents for deposit to the Deputy Chief Financial Officer.
Accompany the gift transmittal with a brief memorandum stating that the gift is pre-decisional.
Include any documentation that accompanied the gift.

constraints.text: Must be done in accordance with the Cash Management Policy, CDC-FM-2009-02.
Must be forwarded within 24 hours of receipt.

steps.text: Submit a copy of the document accepting the gift to OSSAM.
Create a basic record in the on-line property system.
Determine at least annually if there will be excess funds not used within a 12-month period.
Request in writing that the Chief Financial Officer invest the excess funds in interest-bearing obligations of the United States.
Return the monies to the same gift fund account upon liquidation of investments.

preconditions.text: Upon notification of acceptance of the gift by the CIO property custodian.
It is determined that there are excess funds and the amount of these funds is $50,000 or more.
Upon liquidation of investments.

constraints.text: The director's memorandum must include the amount of funds invested, the source of funds, any recommendations regarding purchase, and information concerning when these investments will be redeemed.
Invested gift funds and income from these investments retain the same limitations as the original gifts.
If an investment is redeemed before the maturity date, there may be loss of principal.
The spend plan must be aligned with the investment of the securities and redemption of the securities.

steps.text: Determine whether CDC's interest in accepting a gift outweighs any concern that accepting the gift reflects unfavorably upon the ability of the agency or any employee to carry out responsibilities or official duties in a fair and objective manner, or compromises the integrity or appearance of integrity of a government program or official.
Complete the Gift Acceptance Validity Survey to help analyze the process.
Give considerable scrutiny to all responses in the Gift Acceptance Validity Survey that are answered unfavorably.

constraints.text: If a security is redeemed before the maturity date, there may be loss of principal.
The spend plan must be aligned with the investment of the securities and redemption of the securities.
Gift funds must be received prior to obligation or expenditure.

steps.text: Use CIO letterhead for the conditional gift acceptance letter.
Obtain the donor's signature and date for the 'Use of excess funds acknowledgement'.
Use CIO letterhead for the unconditional gift acceptance letter.

preconditions.text: The CIO Director is the signature authority for the conditional gift.
The CIO Director is the signature authority for the unconditional gift.

constraints.text: Unobligated funds remaining in the account after completion of the project must be deposited in the Unconditional Gift Fund.
